MWCapture SDK  3.3.1.1556
  • Home
  • Development Library
  • Examples
  • Release Notes
GPU Hardware Encoding

Overview

GPU-accelerated H.264 and H.265 encoding is now supported by SDKv3, using the module named mw_venc. It converts video into files with H.264 or H.265 (HEVC) compression for network distribution or space-efficient local storage.

GPU hardware encoder engines for video processing include de-interlacing, resizing, cropping and more. This dedicated encoder supports hardware acceleration of the codecs, thus leaving the CPU free for other operations and the graphics engine bandwidth fully available for video rendering. GPU-accelerated encoding enables higher performance in transcoding applications, and can be used in a flexible variety of motherboard configurations.

mw_venc, new in version 3.3.1, is now integrated into the SDK and will be maintained and updated with the SDK in the future.

Key features

mw_venc supports GPU-accelerated H.264 and H.265 encoding of video captured through Magewell capture devices. This feature is supported on GPUs, including Nvidia, Intel and AMD. Actual performance may vary by the GPU vendor and model, as well as the motherboard configuration. Recommended configuration will be provided in the Performance Test section.

Note that in order to get the best performance, the latest driver for the GPU hardware is required. Please obtain and install the latest driver from your GPU manufacturer.

The features of mw_venc are as follows.

  1. Encode videos into H.264/H.265 files based on hardware.

    Please ensure that your GPU supports H.264 and H.265 hardware encoding. For details, refer to your GPU vendor's hardware documentation.

  2. Use the following interfaces to enumerate and select available hardware encoding devices.

    List GPUs and obtain hardware information using mw_venc_get_gpu_num and mw_venc_get_gpu_info_by_index.

    Create an encoder and start encoding on the GPU specified by the index value of mw_venc_create_by_index.

  3. Customize encoding parameters for your session.

    You can specify custom encoding parameters when creating each encoder instance, allowing you to suit your needs. You can specify profile, bit rate, resolution, frame rate, and even advanced settings including stream control mode and slice number. You can also use the default settings provided by mw_venc_get_default_param to create an encoder. For detailed parameters refer to mw_venc_param_t.

    mw_venc supports changing the encoding profile during encoding. You can obtain the parameter values using mw_venc_get_property, and modify the values during transcoding using mw_vnc_set_propeerty. For encoding parameters that can be modified, refer to mw_venc_property_t. Some parameters may not be modified while encoding is in progress due to the limitation of GPU models or configurations.

  4. Multi-stream encoding on a single GPU.

    You can create several encoders on a GPU using mw_venc_create_by_index. The encoders can be H.264 or H.265 encoders, each configured with different encoder settings such as resolutions and bit rates, which can encode video streams simultaneously.

    GPU capability restricts the number of encoders you can create. To get more informatin, see your GPU vendor's documentation.

  5. Multi-stream encoding on different GPUs

    You can create encoders running on different GPUs using mw_venc_create_by_index. The encoders can be H.264 or H.265 encoders, each configured with different encoder settings such as resolutions and bit rates, which can encode video streams simultaneously. Encoders running on different GPUs work independently without interfering with each other.

System Requirements

Supported OS

Operating systems supported by mw_venc are the same as those of SDKv3. For details, see MWCapture SDKv3 Release Notes.

Ensure that your GPU and its driver supports hardware encoding before calling mw_venc.

Performance Test

Performance represents the quality measured during working. It may vary based on vendors, modules, OS and software versions, and motherboard configurations.

The following table shows the differences between the GPUs when multiple encoders are working simultaneously.

  1. Nvidia Test Result

    Board Format Resolution Input Streams Frame rate
    Quadro K4200 H264 720x480 NV12 16 60fps
    Quadro K4200 H264 1920x1080 NV12 3 60fps
    Quadro K4200 H264 3840x2160 NV12 1 30fps
    Quadro K4200 H264 3840x2160 NV12 2 25fps
    Quadro K4200 H265 - - - Not supported
    Quadro M4000 H264 720x480 NV12 32 60fps
    Quadro M4000 H264 1920x1080 NV12 8 60fps
    Quadro M4000 H264 3840x2160 NV12 1 30fps
    Quadro M4000 H265 720x480 NV12 16 60fps
    Quadro M4000 H265 1920x1080 NV12 2 60fps
    Quadro M4000 H265 3840x2160 NV12 1 20fps
    GeForce GTX 1060 H264 720x480 NV12 2 60fps
    GeForce GTX 1060 H264 720x480 NV12 >2 Not supported
    GeForce GTX 1060 H264 1920x1080 NV12 2 120fps
    GeForce GTX 1060 H264 1920x1080 NV12 >2 Not supported
    GeForce GTX 1060 H264 3840x2160 NV12 2 60fps
    GeForce GTX 1060 H264 3840x2160 NV12 >2 Not supported
    GeForce GTX 1060 H265 720x480 NV12 2 60fps
    GeForce GTX 1060 H265 720x480 NV12 >2 Not supported
    GeForce GTX 1060 H265 1920x1080 NV12 2 120fps
    GeForce GTX 1060 H265 1920x1080 NV12 >2 Not supported
    GeForce GTX 1060 H265 3840x2160 NV12 2 30fps
    GeForce GTX 1060 H265 3840x2160 NV12 >2 Not supported
    GeForce GTX 1080 H264 720x480 NV12 2 60fps
    GeForce GTX 1080 H264 720x480 NV12 >2 Not supported
    GeForce GTX 1080 H264 1920x1080 NV12 2 120fps
    GeForce GTX 1080 H264 1920x1080 NV12 >2 Not supported
    GeForce GTX 1080 H264 3840x2160 NV12 2 60fps
    GeForce GTX 1080 H264 3840x2160 NV12 >2 Not supported
    GeForce GTX 1080 H265 720x480 NV12 2 60fps
    GeForce GTX 1080 H265 720x480 NV12 >2 Not supported
    GeForce GTX 1080 H265 1920x1080 NV12 2 120fps
    GeForce GTX 1080 H265 1920x1080 NV12 >2 Not supported
    GeForce GTX 1080 H265 3840x2160 NV12 2 30fps
    GeForce GTX 1080 H265 3840x2160 NV12 >2 Not supported
    GeForce RTX 2060 H264 720x480 NV12 2 60fps
    GeForce RTX 2060 H264 720x480 NV12 >2 Not supported
    GeForce RTX 2060 H264 1920x1080 NV12 2 60fps
    GeForce RTX 2060 H264 1920x1080 NV12 >2 Not supported
    GeForce GTX 2060 H264 3840x2160 NV12 2 60fps
    GeForce GTX 2060 H264 3840x2160 NV12 >2 Not supported
    GeForce RTX 2060 H265 720x480 NV12 2 60fps
    GeForce RTX 2060 H265 720x480 NV12 >2 Not supported
    GeForce RTX 2060 H265 1920x1080 NV12 2 60fps
    GeForce RTX 2060 H265 1920x1080 NV12 >2 Not supported
    GeForce GTX 2060 H265 3840x2160 NV12 2 60fps
    GeForce GTX 2060 H265 3840x2160 NV12 >2 Not supported

    Maximum of concurrent sessions of Nvidia is restrict to 2. For details, refer to Nvidia GPU Support Matrix.

  2. Intel Test Result

    Board Format Resolution Input Streams Frame rate
    HD530 H264 720x480 NV12 16 60fps
    HD530 H264 1920x1080 NV12 6 60fps
    HD530 H264 1920x1080 NV12 2 120fps
    HD530 H264 3840x2160 NV12 2 60fps
    HD530 H264 3840x2160 NV12 4 30fps
    HD530 H265 720x480 NV12 16 60fps
    HD530 H265 1920x1080 NV12 6 60fps
    HD530 H265 3840x2160 NV12 1 60fps
    HD530 H265 3840x2160 NV12 2 30fps
    HD630 H264 720x480 NV12 16 60fps
    HD630 H264 1920x1080 NV12 8 60fps
    HD630 H264 3840x2160 NV12 2 60fps
    HD630 H264 3840x2160 NV12 4 30fps
    HD630 H265 720x480 NV12 14 60fps
    HD630 H265 1920x1080 NV12 6 60fps
    HD630 H265 3840x2160 NV12 1 60fps
    HD630 H265 3840x2160 NV12 2 30fps

  3. AMD Test Result

    Board Format Resolution Input Streams Frame rate
    AMD R9 380 H264 720x480 NV12 12 60fps
    AMD R9 380 H264 1920x1080 NV12 3 60fps
    AMD R9 380 H264 3840x2160 NV12 1 30fps
    AMD R9 380 H264 3840x2160 NV12 2 25fps
    AMD R9 380 H265 - - - Not supported
    AMD Radeon(TM) Pro WX5100 Graphics H264 720x480 NV12 8 60fps
    AMD Radeon(TM) Pro WX5100 Graphics H264 1920x1080 NV12 2 60fps
    AMD Radeon(TM) Pro WX5100 Graphics H264 1920x1080 NV12 1 120fps
    AMD Radeon(TM) Pro WX5100 Graphics H264 3840x2160 NV12 1 30fps
    AMD Radeon(TM) Pro WX5100 Graphics H265 720x480 NV12 14 60fps
    AMD Radeon(TM) Pro WX5100 Graphics H265 1920x1080 NV12 3 60fps
    AMD Radeon(TM) Pro WX5100 Graphics H265 1920x1080 NV12 1 120fps
    AMD Radeon(TM) Pro WX5100 Graphics H265 3840x2160 NV12 1 30fps
    AMD Radeon(TM) Pro WX5100 Graphics H265 3840x2160 NV12 2 25fps
    AMD Radeon(TM) RX 480 Graphics H264 720x480 NV12 8 60fps
    AMD Radeon(TM) RX 480 Graphics H264 1920x1080 NV12 2 60fps
    AMD Radeon(TM) RX 480 Graphics H264 1920x1080 NV12 1 120fps
    AMD Radeon(TM) RX 480 Graphics H264 3840x2160 NV12 1 30fps
    AMD Radeon(TM) RX 480 Graphics H265 720x480 NV12 6 60fps
    AMD Radeon(TM) RX 480 Graphics H265 1920x1080 NV12 3 60fps
    AMD Radeon(TM) RX 480 Graphics H265 1920x1080 NV12 1 120fps
    AMD Radeon(TM) RX 480 Graphics H265 3840x2160 NV12 1 30fps

    Diagram shows that the H.264 and H.265 encoders running on the same AMD graphics card do not interfer with one another, that each can output at the theoretical maximum frame rates when both are outputting streams simultaneously. Other GPUs such as Nvidia and Intel do not show the same performance. Encoding H.264 and H.265 simultaneously may affect one another and a steady output for each stream cannot be guaranteed.

Guidline

API and data structure

C/C++ interfaces are supported by mw_venc.

Interfaces are as follows.

  • To initialize mw_venc, call: mw_venc_init
  • To enumerate the GPU(s), call: mw_venc_get_gpu_num and mw_venc_get_gpu_info_by_index
  • To create an encoder, call: mw_venc_create_by_index
  • To feed a video data, call: mw_venc_put_frame
  • To obtain parameters of encoder dynamically, call: mw_venc_get_property
  • To set parameters of encoder dynamically, call: mw_venc_set_property
  • To destroy an encoder, call: mw_venc_destory
  • To destroy the mw_venc, call: mw_venc_deinit

The data structures and definitions of functions related to hardware encoding are located in the following files.

  • mw_venc_common.h
  • mw_venc.h
  • mw_venc_common_ex.h
  • mw_venc_ex.h

The data structures and detailed descriptions of functions related to hardware encoding are in the following files.

  • Hardware Encoder Module Structures
  • Hardware Encoder Module Enumeration
  • Hardware Encoder Module Functions
  • Hardware Encoder Module Extension Structures
  • Hardware Encoder Module Extension Enumeration
  • Hardware Encoder Module Extension Functions

Examples

MWVencEncode C++ is provided as an example, which supports capturing, rendering, encoding, and saving videos into H264 or H265 files.
